commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a new Expo/React Native mobile app under `mobile/`, mobile CI, generated app assets, URL-policy tests, and iOS run/proof docs.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a user bug report. The proof gap is high-confidence from the PR body and author comment: real simulator/device proof is still missing.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs maintainer product/security decision and contributor-supplied real iOS proof, so the narrow README fix is not enough to route the PR to automation.

target.title: [codex] add iOS mobile app
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a new `mobile/` Expo app for Crabbox with an iOS-first native shell around `crabbox.sh` and self-hosted Crabbox coordinators.

## What changed

- Scaffolds an Expo SDK 56 TypeScript app under `mobile/`.
- Builds a native WebView client that preserves portal/GitHub OAuth cookies, supports iOS back gestures and pull-to-refresh, and exposes native back, home, reload, share, open-in-browser, and coordinator settings controls.
- Persists a configurable coordinator URL, defaulting to `https://crabbox.sh`.
- Requires HTTPS coordinator sessions by default; development builds allow only loopback HTTP URLs and the WebView no longer whitelists arbitrary cleartext origins.
- Adds branded Crabbox icon/splash assets generated from a tracked SVG source.
- Adds a `Mobile` CI job for install, coordinator URL policy tests, TypeScript, and iOS bundle export.
- Documents Mac/iOS run steps, Xcode setup, Expo dev menu usage, coordinator URL policy, and native prebuild flow.

## User impact

This gives Crabbox a concrete first mobile app surface focused on iOS. Users can launch the Crabbox web portal in a native container, stay signed in through WebView cookies, and switch to a team/self-hosted broker without changing code.

## Validation

- `npm ci` from `mobile/`
- `npm run test:urls` from `mobile/`
- `npm run check` from `mobile/`
- `npx expo export --platform ios --output-dir /tmp/crabbox-mobile-export` from `mobile/`
- `git diff --check`
- GitHub Actions CI run https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/actions/runs/27529710307: `Go`, `Apple VZ`, `Worker`, `Mobile`, `Scripts`, `Docs`, and `Release Check` all passed.
- `npm audit --omit=dev` reports a moderate transitive `uuid` advisory through Expo CLI/config tooling; npm's forced fix would move Expo to an incompatible old Expo version, so the SDK-compatible lockfile is left unchanged.

## Local iOS note

I could not launch the simulator on this machine because `xcode-select -p` points to `/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools`, `xcrun simctl` is unavailable, and there is no `/Applications/Xcode*.app`. The README includes the Xcode selection commands needed before `npm run ios` can open the simulator. Real login-persistence and coordinator-switching proof still needs a Mac with full Xcode plus a redacted test session or device/simulator capture.
